description: Provenance lock for third-party derived skills in the OpenCode Agent Ecosystem. Records upstream sources, commits, licenses, and local adaptations for supply-chain auditability.

entries.name: frontend-design-build
entries.license_note: Upstream repository README states Apache 2.0 for open-source skills. The referenced LICENSE.txt file did not exist at the pinned commit; license assignment is based on repository-level declaration.
modifications_summary: Added Scenario Classification (A-E) with routing rules for existing system vs. greenfield vs. review vs. bugfix scenarios
Added Existing-System-First discovery workflow with token, component, brand, layout, dark mode, motion, form, and navigation audit layers
Added Owner/Approval Gate integration requiring explicit approval for new visual direction, fonts, images, npm dependencies, component libraries, token migration, and navigation restructure

Added structured Design Plan requirement with mandatory sections: product, audience, primary task, existing system, direction, color tokens, typography, layout, signature element, desktop/mobile behavior, all states (loading/empty/error/success/focus/reduced-motion), and open assumptions
Added Assumption marking protocol (ASSUMPTION/EVIDENCE/IMPACT/OWNER_CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED) for missing brief elements — never invent personas, research, or brand data

Added strict implementation rules: semantic HTML, visible focus-visible rings, keyboard operability, prefers-reduced-motion respect, responsive 375px-1280px+, real content or labeled placeholders, explicit states, no fake functionality, no fake APIs, no silent external requests

Added explicit Completion Boundary separating design/build scope from independent review: UX review, design system review, visual regression, and production readiness are owned by separate review agents
Added activation/deactivation rules for OpenCode detector integration
Added Security Boundaries: owner approval for new deps, no runtime network fetch, redaction rules, no production data access
Prohibited self-review and self-approval of UX/accessibility/production claims — these must go through independent review agents

entries.name: ux-flow-review
modifications_summary: Added URL and Deep-Link States section: verify state persistence in URLs, Back/Forward navigation, shareable links
Added Clear Action Labels section: descriptive button labels, consistent terminology, ellipsis for follow-up actions, active voice
Added Focus on First Error section: focus moves to first validation error on form submission, inline errors with aria-live
Added Unsaved Changes Protection section: beforeunload warnings, navigation guards
Added Mobile and Touch Flows section: minimum 44px touch targets, 16px input font size for iOS, touch-action manipulation, safe area insets
Added Recovery Paths section: actionable error messages, empty state invitations, undo for irreversible actions
Added Locale-Aware Content section: Intl.* formatting, Accept-Language detection, translate=no for brand terms

entries.name: ui-design-system-review
modifications_summary: Added Image Dimensions and Layout Shift section: explicit width/height on img, aspect-ratio, CLS prevention
Added Text Overflow and Long Content section: handling very short to very long content, text-overflow, line-clamp, user-generated content resilience
Added Safe Area Support section: env(safe-area-inset-*), viewport-fit, fixed element notches
Added Dark Mode Metadata section: meta theme-color, color-scheme, native select styling, forced-colors mode
Added Locale and Intl.* Formatting section: tabular-nums, non-breaking spaces, locale-aware formatting
Added Hydration Safety section: input focus preservation, SSR/client mismatch prevention, skeleton dimensions, Suspense minimum visible time
Added Reduced Motion section: prefers-reduced-motion variants, no autoplay, scroll-behavior gating
Added Touch Target Sizes section: 44px mobile / 24px desktop minimums, hit target expansion, checkbox/radio shared targets
Added Native Form Elements in Dark Mode section: explicit background-color, color-scheme on form elements, Windows Chrome testing

entries.name: playwright-visual-review
modifications_summary: Updated WCAG target from 2.1 AA to 2.2 AA
Added axe configuration details: wcag22aa, wcag21aa, best-practice tag sets
Added explicit documentation of axe limitations (~30-40% coverage, cannot verify focus order, alt text quality, link purpose, error recovery, content readability)
Added mandatory disclaimer: Automated axe scan is partial verification; manual keyboard and screen reader testing still required
Added WCAG 2.2 contrast ratio for UI components and graphical objects (3:1)
Added focus order verification requirement (separate from axe)
Added reduced motion verification for no-autoplay and instant transitions
Added rule: missing baselines must be marked NEEDS_HUMAN_REVIEW, never auto-approved
Added GitHub comment gate: never post autonomously — requires orchestrator/owner approval
